# The Campaign | 15-Day Challenge Host Guide

## Build Your First AI Marketing Agent: Host and Trainer Guide

<callout icon="📅" color="blue_bg">
	**Challenge:** Sun Jun 21 to Sun Jul 5, 2026 | **Platform:** Nas.io | **Host:** Matt | **Trainers:** TBC (recruiting) | All times **SGT**
</callout>

---

<callout icon="🧭" color="blue_bg">
	**Your role in one line.** Each day you deliver a short lesson (recorded or live) that teaches one piece of the agent and sets one 30-minute task. By Day 15 every member has a working AI marketing agent that hands them a fresh content idea and a ready-to-run prompt every morning. The host (Matt) replies to submissions daily and runs the gates.
</callout>

<callout icon="🎯" color="green_bg">
	**The destination (show this on Day 1).** A member types "today's idea" and the agent replies with a fresh, on-trend content idea for their business plus a ready-to-run prompt to make it. That is the whole promise. No more blank page.
</callout>

## How to Use This Guide

Every day follows the same three parts: **one lesson** (10 to 15 minutes), **one task** (about 30 minutes), and **one community post** in that day's channel as proof of work. All dates and facts come from Source of Truth.

<table fit-page-width="true" header-row="true">
<tr color="blue_bg">
<td>Color</td>
<td>Type</td>
<td>Meaning</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Gray</td>
<td>Standard Day</td>
<td>No submission required</td>
</tr>
<tr color="orange_bg">
<td>Orange</td>
<td>Submission Day</td>
<td>Graded for participation</td>
</tr>
<tr color="red_bg">
<td>Red</td>
<td>Gate Day</td>
<td>Required to qualify or graduate</td>
</tr>
<tr color="purple_bg">
<td>Purple</td>
<td>Graduation</td>
<td>The finish line</td>
</tr>
</table>

<callout icon="🚨" color="red_bg">
	**Two gates.** Day 8 (the 2-minute video) must be posted to qualify. Day 14 (the final submission) must be posted to graduate. Missing either removes the member from the graduation and competition track. They keep their agent and can still attend the Summit.
</callout>

<callout icon="🎬" color="gray_bg">
	**Film order.** Record Day 1 and Day 2 first, then the rest in order. Each lesson video is 10 to 15 minutes (gate days are shorter). Pair every video with that day's WhatsApp nudge and Feedback reply.
</callout>

---

## Week 1: Set Up the Brain and Build the Engine (Jun 21 to 27)

---

### Day 1 - Orientation: What You Are Building

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	Reframe. Most people think posting online means sitting at a blank screen and forcing an idea. An AI marketing agent flips that. It is YOUR agent, set up once, that every morning hands you a fresh content idea for your business and a ready-to-run prompt to make it. Not a chatbot you re-explain yourself to every time. A teammate that already knows your business and shows up with the idea.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- The shift: from "what should I post?" to "which of these ideas do I post?"
	- What they will have by Jul 5: an agent that gives one idea plus one ready-to-run prompt every day
	- How the 15 days work: 30 minutes a day, one task, one post in the day's channel
	- The two gates they cannot miss: Day 8 (2-minute video) and Day 14 (final agent)
	- The prize: $1,000 in Nas.com credits, announced live at the Summit on Wed Jul 8. Must be present to win.
	- The only thing they need: a Claude Project (the agent's home). No code, no design tools.
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 10 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "I was talking to a member who opens Instagram every morning, stares at the box, and closes it. A whole year of not posting. Sound familiar? That stops this week."
	**Show on screen:** open Claude and run the finished agent. Type "today's idea" and show the idea plus the ready-to-run prompt it returns. Let them see the destination on Day 1.
	**Key line to say:** "By Jul 5 you'll type two words, today's idea, and your agent hands you a fresh idea for your business plus the prompt to make it. No more blank screen."
	**Close with:** "Your task takes 10 minutes. Create your Project, say hello, tell us what you sell. That's it. See you tomorrow."
	**Tone:** warm, excited, non-technical. This is a mindset video, not a how-to.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (10 minutes):**
	1. Join the community space (link in your welcome email)
	2. Open Claude at claude.ai and click "Projects" in the left sidebar
	3. Click "New Project" and name it "My Marketing Agent" (leave it empty for now)
	4. Post a hello in #Day1 with one line: what you sell and who you sell it to
	No product photos, no tech, no agent yet. Just show up and say what you sell.
</callout>

---

### Day 2 - Name Your Business

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	Your agent is only as smart as what it knows about your business. Before it can give a single good idea, it needs the basics, in three sentences. Not an essay. Three sentences forces clarity: what you sell, who it is for, and the one thing that makes it yours.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- Why three sentences: clarity beats length. The agent needs signal, not a paragraph.
	- The three sentences: what you sell, who it is for, the one thing that makes it different
	- Paste Template 1 (Project setup) as the Project's custom instructions first
	- Garbage in, garbage out. A vague business gives vague ideas.
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "The members who get the best ideas later are the ones who get specific today. The ones who stay vague get vague ideas all the way to Day 15."
	**Show on screen:** paste Template 1 into the Project instructions, then type a clean 3-sentence business description for the example business, live.
	**Key line to say:** "Garbage in, garbage out. The clearer your business, the better the ideas. Three sentences, that is it."
	**Close with:** "Paste the template, write your three sentences inside your Project, post them in #Day2. Takes 15 minutes."
	**Tone:** practical, encouraging. Make them commit to specifics.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (15 minutes):**
	1. Open your "My Marketing Agent" Project from Day 1
	2. Paste Template 1 (Project setup) into the Project Instructions (see Agent Templates)
	3. Write your business in 3 sentences inside the Project: what you sell, who you sell it to, the one thing that makes you different
	4. Post your 3 sentences in #Day2
</callout>

---

### Day 3 - Build the Business Profile

<callout icon="🟠" color="orange_bg">
	**SUBMISSION 1**
</callout>

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	Three sentences got us started. Today we give the agent a real profile: product, price, the one differentiator, the outcome the customer actually buys, and where you sell. This is the foundation every future idea is built on.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- The 5 lines: product, price, the ONE thing competitors do not have, the result the customer buys, the channels
	- Hammer the differentiator: name the one thing only you do
	- Use Template 2 and paste it into the Project
	- Specific beats generic. A thin profile is the number one cause of boring ideas later.
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "The number one reason an agent gives boring ideas is a thin profile. The members who write a sharp differentiator today get sharp ideas all the way to Day 15."
	**Show on screen:** fill Template 2 live for the example business, thinking out loud on the differentiator line. Compare a vague differentiator to a specific one.
	**Key line to say:** "Name the one thing only you do. If a competitor could copy and paste your profile, it is not specific enough yet."
	**Close with:** "Fill all 5 lines of Template 2 into your Project. Post it in #Day3. Takes 25 minutes."
	**Tone:** practical, decisive. Help them commit.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (25 minutes):**
	1. Open your Project
	2. Paste Template 2 (business profile) from Agent Templates
	3. Fill in: what you sell, your price, the ONE thing competitors do not have, the result your customer buys, where you sell
	4. Post your profile in #Day3
	This is Submission 1, graded for participation.
</callout>

---

### Day 4 - Teach It Your Customer

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	Ideas only work if they land with a real person. Today we tell the agent exactly who that person is: who they are, their main problem, where they spend time online, and the words they use for that problem.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- The 5-part customer profile: who they are, their main problem, what they want instead, where they hang out, the exact words they use
	- Review mining: paste a few competitor reviews or comments into Claude and ask what the buyer cares about
	- The sharper the pain, the sharper the ideas
	- Use Template 3 and add it to the Project
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "I used to write posts that sounded like every other store. Then I started using my customer's own words. Everything changed."
	**Show on screen:** inside the Project, paste 5 customer reviews or comments and ask Claude to build a buyer profile. Show the output and point at the exact phrases.
	**Key line to say:** "Write down the words your customer actually uses for their problem. That is gold for your agent."
	**Close with:** "Fill Template 3, or paste reviews and let Claude draft it. Add it to your Project. Post in #Day4. Takes 25 minutes."
	**Tone:** show, do not tell. The live demo is the lesson.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (25 minutes):**
	1. Find 5 to 10 reviews or comments from customers like yours (Amazon, Etsy, Reddit, anywhere)
	2. Open your Project and paste Template 3 (ideal customer)
	3. Fill it in, or paste the reviews and ask Claude: "Based on these, build my ideal customer: who they are, their main problem, where they hang out, and the words they use."
	4. Post your customer profile in #Day4
</callout>

---

### Day 5 - Teach It Your Voice

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	Right now your agent sounds like everyone's agent. Today we make it sound like you, by feeding it two or three things you have already written and the simple rules of your voice.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- Paste 2 to 3 real samples (posts, captions, emails, even a voice-note transcript)
	- Define the voice in 3 words, plus how it does NOT sound
	- Test it: ask the agent to write one line and check the vibe
	- Use Template 4
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "The fastest way to spot an AI post is that it sounds like nobody. Today we fix that for good."
	**Show on screen:** paste 2 to 3 samples into Template 4, add the voice rules, then ask the agent to write one sentence and react to it live.
	**Key line to say:** "Make it sound like you, not a brand. Ask it to write one line and trust your ear."
	**Close with:** "Add your samples and voice rules to the Project. Post the one line it wrote in #Day5. Takes 20 minutes."
	**Tone:** light and fun. This is the day the agent starts to feel personal.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (20 minutes):**
	1. Find 2 to 3 things you have written that sound like you
	2. Open your Project and paste Template 4 (voice)
	3. Add your samples, then write your voice in 3 words and how you do NOT sound
	4. Ask the agent to write one sentence in your voice
	5. Post that sentence in #Day5 with: "This is my agent in my voice."
</callout>

---

### Day 6 - Build the Idea Engine

<callout icon="🟠" color="orange_bg">
	**SUBMISSION 2 | THE HEART OF THE CHALLENGE**
</callout>

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	This is the engine. Everything before today was setup. Everything after just sharpens it. The core instruction makes the agent look at what is trending or top of mind for your customer, connect it to your product, give one idea in a sentence, name the best format, and write a ready-to-run prompt. Spend the most time here.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- The 5 steps of the engine: scan a trend or pain, connect it to the product, one-sentence idea, best format, ready-to-run prompt
	- Use Template 5. Walk it line by line.
	- No generic "post a tip" ideas. Every idea names the product or the customer.
	- This is the first working agent draft. It does not have to be perfect, it has to run.
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 10 minutes, go slow)**
	**Open with story:** "Everything so far has been feeding the agent. Today it gives something back. This is the day your agent goes from a profile to a working tool."
	**Show on screen:** paste Template 5, run it once, then improve one weak idea live so they see the editing loop. React honestly to what it gives you.
	**Key line to say:** "This is the engine. If today felt big, that is because it is. Tomorrow we just sharpen it."
	**Close with:** "Build the engine, run it once, post the idea it gave you in #Day6. Takes 30 minutes."
	**Tone:** patient and clear. Move slowly. This is the make-or-break day.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (30 minutes):**
	1. Open your Project and paste Template 5 (the idea engine)
	2. Read it once so you understand the 5 steps
	3. Run it: ask the agent for "today's idea"
	4. Post the idea it gave you in #Day6 with one line on whether you would actually post it
	This is Submission 2, your first working agent draft. If the idea feels generic, your Day 3 profile or Day 4 customer needs more detail. Go back and sharpen them.
</callout>

---

### Day 7 - First Ideas

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	One idea is luck. Today the engine repeats and you learn to judge. Run it three times, keep the one you would actually post, and cut the rest. Judgment is the real skill.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- Run the engine 3 times to see range
	- The gut check: "which one would I genuinely post today?"
	- Keep one, cut the rest. More is not better.
	- Trusting your taste is part of the build.
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "The agent will give you good ideas and forgettable ones. The skill is not generating, it is choosing."
	**Show on screen:** generate 3 ideas for the example business and talk through, out loud, why you keep one and cut two.
	**Key line to say:** "Trust your gut on which one you would actually post. That instinct is the skill."
	**Close with:** "Generate 3 ideas, keep the best, post it in #Day7. Takes 20 minutes."
	**Tone:** confident, encouraging their taste.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (20 minutes):**
	1. Open your Project and run the idea engine 3 times
	2. Read the 3 ideas. Pick the one you would actually post today.
	3. Post your favorite in #Day7 with one line on why you chose it
</callout>

---

## Week 2: Make It Useful, Prove It, and Graduate (Jun 28 to Jul 5)

---

### Day 8 - Gate 1: The 2-Minute Video

<callout icon="🔴" color="red_bg">
	**SUBMISSION 3 | QUALIFICATION GATE**
</callout>

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	Halfway. No new material today. This is the un-quitting move. A 2-minute phone video separates the builders from the watchers, and it is the one gate that keeps people from quietly falling off.
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 3 minutes)**
	**No teaching today.** Just encouragement and clarity.
	**Say:** "Day 8. Halfway. If you have kept up, you are in great shape. If you have fallen behind, this is your moment to catch up. One video. 2 minutes. That is all it takes to stay on the graduation track."
	**Emphasize:** "No video means no graduation, no competition, no $1,000 in credits. Phone camera. No editing. Just talk."
	**Tone:** warm but firm. Make the gate feel real, not scary.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (5 minutes):**
	Record a 2-minute video on your phone. No editing. No script. No polish.
	Show two things:
	1. Your agent producing one idea on screen
	2. Say out loud the one thing tripping you up right now
	Post the video in #Day8 by 11:59pm SGT.
	**This is required to qualify for graduation.** No video means no graduation and no competition. Every video gets a personal reply from the host team.
</callout>

---

### Day 9 - Idea to Ready-to-Run Prompt

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	An idea is nice. A ready-to-run prompt is useful. Today the agent turns each idea into a prompt that already carries your product, your customer, and your voice, so you just paste and run. This is the moment it goes from a toy to a tool.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- The prompt must already include the product, the customer, and the voice
	- Use Template 6
	- The test: can you paste the prompt and get usable content with zero edits?
	- Toy to tool. This is when the agent starts saving real time.
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "An idea still leaves you with work to do. A ready-to-run prompt does the work. Today we close that gap."
	**Show on screen:** take yesterday's idea and have the agent write the paste-ready prompt with Template 6. Paste it and show the content it produces.
	**Key line to say:** "Now it hands you something you can paste. Toy to tool."
	**Close with:** "Add the prompt step to your agent. Post a before and after in #Day9. Takes 25 minutes."
	**Tone:** practical, momentum-building.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (25 minutes):**
	1. Open your Project and paste Template 6 (idea to prompt)
	2. Take one of your ideas and ask the agent for the ready-to-run prompt
	3. Paste that prompt back in and see the content it makes
	4. Post the idea and the prompt it produced in #Day9
</callout>

---

### Day 10 - One Idea, Three Formats

<callout icon="🟠" color="orange_bg">
	**SUBMISSION 4**
</callout>

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	One idea is a week of content if you reshape it. Today you teach the agent to turn a single idea into a short post, a short-video script, and a caption, all in your voice.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- 3 formats from 1 idea: a short post, a 20-second reel or TikTok script, a one-line caption
	- Use Template 7
	- Keep the voice consistent across all three
	- This is how members never run dry on content
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "Most people get one idea and make one post. Then they are stuck again tomorrow. We are going to fix that math."
	**Show on screen:** run Template 7 on one idea and show the three outputs side by side, all in the example brand's voice.
	**Key line to say:** "One idea, three formats. That is how you never run dry."
	**Close with:** "Make your agent output one idea in 3 formats. Post all three in #Day10. Takes 30 minutes."
	**Tone:** energizing. The three-in-one is the wow moment.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (30 minutes):**
	1. Open your Project and paste Template 7 (three formats)
	2. Take one idea and ask the agent for a short post, a reel or TikTok script, and a one-line caption
	3. Check that all three sound like you
	4. Post all three in #Day10
	This is Submission 4, graded for participation.
</callout>

---

### Day 11 - Make It Proactive

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	Until now you have been asking. Today the agent starts offering. You set the routine so that when you say "today's idea," the agent runs the whole flow automatically: one idea, the best format, and a ready-to-run prompt, with no questions first.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- Use Template 8 to set the trigger phrase "today's idea"
	- If the Claude plan supports scheduled runs, set it to fire each morning. If not, they just type the two words.
	- The agent should not ask questions first. It should deliver.
	- This is the promise made real: no more blank page.
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "The best tools do not wait to be asked. From today, your agent greets you with an idea before you have had your coffee."
	**Show on screen:** set up Template 8 and trigger it with "today's idea" so they see the full automatic output appear in one shot.
	**Key line to say:** "Now it greets you with an idea. That is the whole promise, no more blank page."
	**Close with:** "Set up your daily routine. Post the first automatic idea in #Day11. Takes 25 minutes."
	**Tone:** exciting. This is the payoff of all the setup.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (25 minutes):**
	1. Open your Project and paste Template 8 (make it proactive)
	2. Set "today's idea" as the trigger that runs the whole flow automatically
	3. Type "today's idea" and watch it deliver one idea, the best format, and the prompt
	4. Post that result in #Day11
	If your plan supports scheduling, set it to run each morning. If not, just type the two words when you sit down.
</callout>

---

### Day 12 - Tighten It

<callout icon="🟠" color="orange_bg">
	**SUBMISSION 5**
</callout>

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	Polish day. Today we hunt down anything generic and kill it. Every idea the agent gives should name your product or your customer. If an idea could belong to any business, it belongs to none.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- How to spot a generic idea: it could be posted by any business in any niche
	- Rewrite the engine instruction so the agent always names the product or the customer
	- Refine Template 5 (the idea engine) with the lessons from Days 9 to 11
	- The bar: every idea is unmistakably yours
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "I read a member's ideas and they were fine, but they could have come from any account. Fine is the enemy. Today we make every idea unmistakably theirs."
	**Show on screen:** find one generic idea live and tighten the instructions until the next idea names the product and the customer.
	**Key line to say:** "If an idea could belong to any business, it belongs to none. Make it yours."
	**Close with:** "Polish your instructions so every idea is specific. Post a before and after in #Day12. Takes 25 minutes."
	**Tone:** sharp, quality-focused.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (25 minutes):**
	1. Run your agent and find one idea that feels generic
	2. Edit your engine instruction so it must name your product or your customer in every idea
	3. Run it again and confirm the new idea is specific
	4. Post the before and after in #Day12
	This is Submission 5, graded for participation.
</callout>

---

### Day 13 - Prove It for a Week

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	Today we prove it. Run the agent 7 times in one sitting and look at a full week of ideas at once. This is where members stop doubting and start trusting the thing they built.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- Run the engine 7 times back to back
	- Skim a full week of ideas and pick a favorite to bring into the final
	- Seeing a week at once is what builds real confidence
	- This favorite becomes the demo idea in the Day 14 submission
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 8 minutes)**
	**Open with story:** "One good idea is exciting. Seven in a row is when it hits you: this thing actually works, and it is yours."
	**Show on screen:** generate 7 ideas in one sitting for the example business and pick a favorite out loud.
	**Key line to say:** "Seven ideas in one sitting. That is the proof it works. Bring your favorite to tomorrow."
	**Close with:** "Generate a week of ideas, pick your favorite, post it in #Day13. Takes 25 minutes."
	**Tone:** proud, building toward the finish.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to do (25 minutes):**
	1. Open your Project and run the idea engine 7 times
	2. Read the full week of ideas
	3. Pick your favorite. You will use it as the demo in tomorrow's submission.
	4. Post your favorite in #Day13
</callout>

---

### Day 14 - Gate 2: Final Submission

<callout icon="🔴" color="red_bg">
	**SUBMISSION 6 | FINAL GATE, REQUIRED TO GRADUATE**
</callout>

<details color="blue_bg">
<summary>Lesson</summary>
	This is it. Last build day. Today you submit your finished agent, and your submission is your competition entry. Three things, in three separate boxes on the form. A link or a file works in every box. Do not polish, submit.
</details>

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>Key Teaching Points</summary>
	- The 3 boxes: (1) the agent, as a link or a 60 to 90 second screen recording, (2) the one-page brief (Template 9), (3) one demo idea and its prompt
	- Every box takes a link OR a file. Video links are welcome.
	- The quality bar: a judge can run the agent and see it produce a real idea and prompt
	- Deadline 11:59pm SGT. Submit and you graduate. Miss it and you can still attend the Summit but you are out of the competition.
</details>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 5 minutes)**
	**Say:** "Last build day. Today you submit, and your submission is your competition entry. Three boxes: your agent, your one-page brief, and one demo idea with its prompt."
	**Show on screen:** fill the one-page brief with Template 9, record a 60 to 90 second screen capture of the agent producing an idea, and walk through submitting all three boxes.
	**Emphasize:** "11:59pm SGT. A link or a file works in every box. Do not polish. A working agent beats a perfect plan you never send. Submit and you graduate."
	**Close with:** "Submit your three boxes in #Day14. I will be reviewing every one."
	**Tone:** clear, calm, decisive. Get them to send it.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**What to submit (30 minutes):**
	Submit three things in #Day14 by 11:59pm SGT, in three separate boxes on the form:
	1. **Your agent** (a link to your Claude Project, or a 60 to 90 second screen recording of it producing an idea)
	2. **Your one-page brief** (Template 9: what it does, who it is for, how it works)
	3. **One demo idea and its prompt** (paste the favorite from Day 13)
	A link or a file works in every box. **This is the final gate and your competition entry.** Submit and you graduate.
</callout>

---

### Day 15 - Graduation

<callout icon="🎓" color="purple_bg">
	**GRADUATION DAY | Sun Jul 5, 2026**
</callout>

<callout icon="🎬" color="red_bg">
	**Trainer Video Brief (film under 5 minutes)**
	**Say:** "You did it. Most people never start. You built a working AI marketing agent in 15 days. Here is what happens next."
	**Announce:** the graduation list. Name every graduate.
	**Share:** 2 or 3 standout agents (with permission).
	**Give:** the Summit details. Wed Jul 8, 11:00pm to 2:00am SGT. What to bring, what to expect, and that winners are announced live.
	**Close with:** "You built the agent. The Summit is where you see what is possible next, and where we announce the winners. Be there live. See you Wednesday."
	**Tone:** celebration, pride, momentum into the Summit.
</callout>

<callout icon="📋" color="green_bg">
	**Checkpoint Description (what students see)**
	**No task today. You graduated.** Rest.
	Post one sentence in #Day15: the best idea your agent has given you so far.
	**What to bring to the AI Marketing Agent Summit (Wed Jul 8):**
	- Your agent, open and ready to run
	- Your product details
	- Your energy. Winners are announced live, so be present to win.
</callout>

---

## Trainer FAQ

<details color="gray_bg">
<summary>The questions you will hit</summary>
	- **Are the daily lessons live or pre-recorded?** Pre-recorded short videos posted each day; the host (Matt) replies to submissions live.
	- **Do I grade submissions?** No. The host replies and grades daily using the Feedback doc. You teach and set the task.
	- **How long should each lesson be?** 10 to 15 minutes teaching, then a 30-minute task.
	- **What Claude plan do students need?** A Claude account with Projects. [ team to confirm: free plan, or paid? ]
	- **A student's ideas feel generic, what do I tell them?** Their Day 3 profile and Day 4 customer are not detailed enough. Send them back. Day 12 is a polish day.
	- **Someone missed a gate, are they out?** Off the track, but they can still attend the Summit and keep their agent. Offer a 1:1 if close.
	- **What if I cannot demo a step live?** Use the exact prompts in Agent Templates. Paste and run.
	- **Where do people drop off?** Around the middle. Protect Day 6 and hammer the Day 8 gate.
	- **Where do students post?** The #Day1 to #Day15 channels.
	- **What is submitted and when is it graded?** 6 graded days: Days 3, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14. Day 8 and Day 14 are the gates.
</details>

## Teaching guidelines (every session)

<callout icon="✅" color="gray_bg">
	1. End every lesson with the same three things: what to do, how long it takes, what to post.
	2. Realistic outcomes only. "Your first agent," never "automate your whole business" or "10x your revenue."
	3. Plain language. If a first-timer would not get it, cut it.
	4. Point them to the day's template in Agent Templates.
	5. Protect Day 6 (the idea engine). It is the heart. Go slow.
</callout>

---

*Pair with: Source of Truth (the facts), Agent Templates (paste-ready text), WhatsApp Daily Nudges, and Feedback Daily Replies. AI SCHOOL. NAS.com. CONFIDENTIAL.*
